title: pyrus pyraster subsp. mariana
intro: Pyrus pyraster subsp. mariana, also known as the wild pear or mountain pear, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, including diarrhea and dysentery, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pyrus pyraster subsp. mariana include its ability to soothe stomach problems, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Pyrus pyraster subsp. mariana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its bark and leaves are used in traditional folk medicine.
